zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II於1459年5月15日出生在蘇格蘭度福利爾莫頓時，他的父親James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas33歲，母親Princess Joan Stewart30歲。他去世於1513年9月9日在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年54歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II于1459年5月15日出生在苏格兰度福利爾莫頓时，他的父亲James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas33岁，母亲Princess Joan Stewart30岁。他去世于1513年9月9日在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gordon,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Gordon in Berwickshire, named with Welsh gor ‘spacious’ + din ‘fort’.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Gourdon in Saône-et-Loire, so called from the Gallo-Roman personal name Gordus + the locative suffix -o, -ōnis.
English (of Norman origin): alternatively, said to be a nickname from a diminutive of Old French gourd ‘heavy, dull, sluggish’ (compare 8 below).

variants.name: Gordin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Eyal, Moisey, Yakov, Zalman, Erez, Faina, Haya, Mendel.  Russian Leonid, Aleksandr, Boris, Feiga, Genady.
Jewish (from Russia): variant of   Gordon   5.
French: variant of   Gourdin  . This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Gordan
meanings: German: variant of Jordan . The surname Gordan is very rare in Germany.
Irish (Wexford and Sligo): adopted as an Anglicized form of Mag Mhuirneacháin ‘son of Muirneachán’, a personal name from a diminutive of muirneach ‘lovable’.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Simon , now rare, but more common in the Middle Ages, when it gave rise to the surnames Simms and Simpson.

variants.name: Simon
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II was born on 15 May 1459, in Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, his father, James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas, was 33 and his mother, Princess Joan Stewart, was 30. He died on 9 September 1513, in Branxton, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II est né le 15 mai 1459, à Morton, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, son père, James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Princess Joan Stewart, avait 30 ans. Il est décédé le 9 septembre 1513, à Branxton, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II am 15. Mai 1459 in Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas 33 und seine Mutter Princess Joan Stewart 30. Er starb am 9. September 1513 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Branxton, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II è nato il il 15 maggio 1459 nel Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, il suo padre, James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Princess Joan Stewart, aveva 30 anni. Morì il 9 settembre 1513, nel Branxton, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 54 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II(이)가 1459년 5월 15일 스컷랜드 드프리실르 모르턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Princess Joan Stewart(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1513년 9월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 브랜스턴에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.15 May 1459–9 Sep 1513: 15 de maio de 1459–9 de setembro de 1513
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II nasceu em 15 de maio de 1459, em Morton, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, seu pai, James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Princess Joan Stewart, tinha 30 anos. Ele faleceu em 9 de setembro de 1513, em Branxton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Gordon, Lord of Lochinvar II родился 15 мая 1459 г. в местности мортон, думфриессхир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени James Douglas Lord of DalKeith, First Earl Morton, Nineth Earl Douglas было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Princess Joan Stewart, 30. Он умер 9 сентября 1513 г. в местности бранкстон, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 54 лет.

Murió el 9 de septiembre de 1513, en Branxton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 54 años.
